title: Europe Faces Unseasonal Heatwave, Breaking Records and Raising Concerns
subtitle: Early summer temperatures lead to health warnings and tragic incidents across Western Europe.

summary.en: Western Europe is currently experiencing an unusually early and intense heatwave, with countries like the UK, France, Spain, and Ireland reporting record-breaking May temperatures. This extreme weather, driven by a 'heat dome' and made worse by climate change, has led to serious health warnings and several deaths, including drownings. Scientists note that Europe is warming faster than the global average, making such heat events more frequent and severe. Governments are responding with health alerts and restrictions, while experts warn that these conditions are becoming the 'new normal' much sooner than expected. The situation highlights the urgent need for countries to adapt to a changing climate.
summary.zh: 西歐目前正經歷一波異常提早且強烈的熱浪，英國、法國、西班牙和愛爾蘭等國通報五月份氣溫創下歷史新高。這種極端天氣由「熱穹現象」驅動，並因氣候變遷而惡化，已導致嚴重的健康警報和數起死亡事件，包括溺水。科學家指出，歐洲的暖化速度快於全球平均，使得此類熱浪事件更加頻繁和嚴重。各國政府正透過健康警報和限制措施應對，而專家警告，這些情況正比預期更快地成為「新常態」。這種情況凸顯了各國迫切需要適應不斷變化的氣候。

paragraphs.en: Western Europe is currently experiencing an unusually strong and early heatwave, with many countries reporting record-breaking temperatures for May. This extreme weather has led to health warnings and tragic incidents across the region, causing concern among experts and the public.
paragraphs.zh: 西歐目前正經歷一波異常強烈且提早報到的熱浪，許多國家通報五月份氣溫創下歷史新高。這種極端天氣已導致該地區發布健康警報並發生多起悲劇事件，引起專家和民眾的擔憂。

paragraphs.en: According to BBC News, temperatures in the UK passed 35°C on Tuesday, which is more than 2°C higher than the previous May record before this year. The Met Office described this heat as “exceptional even in the middle of summer, let alone spring.” France is also facing an unprecedented early-season heatwave, with hundreds of heat records broken, Météo-France reports. Ireland's May temperature record has been broken by over 1°C, while Germany, Italy, Spain, and Switzerland have all seen unusually hot conditions for spring, BBC News noted.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司新聞網 (BBC News) 報導，英國週二氣溫超過攝氏 35 度，比今年以前五月份的歷史紀錄高出攝氏 2 度以上。英國氣象局 (Met Office) 形容這次高溫「即使在盛夏也屬異常，更何況是春天」。法國也面臨前所未有的提早熱浪，法國氣象局 (Météo-France) 報導，數百個高溫紀錄被打破。愛爾蘭五月份的氣溫紀錄被打破超過攝氏 1 度，而德國、義大利、西班牙和瑞士也都出現了春天異常炎熱的情況，英國廣播公司新聞網指出。

paragraphs.en: The immediate cause of this heatwave is a “heat dome,” where an area of high pressure becomes stuck over Europe, trapping warm air underneath, according to BBC News and NPR News. This hot air has come from northern Africa, as Deutsche Welle (DW) Top reported. However, scientists are clear that human-caused climate change, largely from burning coal, oil, and gas, has made this heat much more intense. The Copernicus climate service states that Europe has been warming by 0.56°C per decade over the last 30 years, which is more than twice the global average, BBC News highlighted. Richard Betts, head of climate impacts research at the Met Office, told BBC News that when heatwaves happen, they are more severe because they are building on an already warming climate.
paragraphs.zh: 英國廣播公司新聞網和美國國家公共廣播電台 (NPR News) 報導，這次熱浪的直接原因是「熱穹現象」，即一個高氣壓區滯留在歐洲上空，將暖空氣困在下方。德國之聲 (DW) 報導，這些熱空氣來自北非。然而，科學家們明確指出，主要由燃燒煤炭、石油和天然氣引起的人為氣候變遷，已使這次高溫更加劇烈。哥白尼氣候服務中心 (Copernicus climate service) 表示，過去 30 年來，歐洲每十年升溫攝氏 0.56 度，是全球平均的兩倍多，英國廣播公司新聞網強調。英國氣象局氣候影響研究主管理查·貝茨 (Richard Betts) 告訴英國廣播公司新聞網，當熱浪發生時，由於氣候已經在變暖，它們會變得更加嚴重。

paragraphs.en: Many places have seen temperatures not just break but “smash” previous records. For example, The Guardian Environment reported that the UK recorded its highest ever May temperature for the second day in a row, with thermometers hitting 35.1°C in London. This broke the previous May record of 32.8°C, which had stood since 1922. NPR News added that London also experienced a rare “tropical night,” meaning temperatures did not fall below 20°C. In France, the national heat index, which measures the average temperature across the country, reached 24.8°C, surpassing previous records, according to The Guardian World. Météo-France said this heatwave could last through the week, with some areas possibly reaching 39°C.
paragraphs.zh: 許多地方的氣溫不僅打破了，甚至「粉碎」了之前的紀錄。例如，《衛報環境版》(The Guardian Environment) 報導，英國連續第二天創下五月份歷史最高氣溫，倫敦的溫度計達到攝氏 35.1 度。這打破了自 1922 年以來保持的五月份攝氏 32.8 度的紀錄。美國國家公共廣播電台補充說，倫敦還經歷了一個罕見的「熱帶夜」，意味著氣溫沒有降到攝氏 20 度以下。根據《衛報世界版》(The Guardian World) 報導，法國的國家熱度指數（衡量全國平均氣溫）達到攝氏 24.8 度，超越了之前的紀錄。法國氣象局表示，這次熱浪可能會持續一週，部分地區可能達到攝氏 39 度。

paragraphs.en: The unseasonal heat has led to serious consequences. The UK Health Security Agency issued amber health alerts for large parts of England, warning of potential health risks, especially for older people, NPR News reported. The National Fire Chiefs Council also issued a water safety warning after several deaths over a bank holiday weekend. The Guardian Environment and NPR News reported that at least four teenagers died in apparent drownings in UK lakes and reservoirs, and a man in his 60s died trying to save family members in the sea. In France, government spokesperson Maud Bregeon told TF1 television that there have been seven deaths linked directly or indirectly to the heat, with five of these being drownings, according to The Guardian World and NPR News. Many of these drownings happened because people were trying to cool down, and the heatwave struck before lifeguards were fully watching popular beaches, increasing risks, NPR News noted.
paragraphs.zh: 這波不合時宜的熱浪已導致嚴重後果。美國國家公共廣播電台報導，英國衛生安全局 (UK Health Security Agency) 對英格蘭大部分地區發布了琥珀色健康警報，警告潛在的健康風險，特別是對老年人。國家消防總長理事會 (National Fire Chiefs Council) 在一個銀行假日週末發生數起死亡事件後，也發布了水域安全警告。《衛報環境版》和美國國家公共廣播電台報導，至少有四名青少年在英國湖泊和水庫中疑似溺水身亡，一名六十多歲的男子在海中試圖營救家人時喪生。根據《衛報世界版》和美國國家公共廣播電台報導，法國政府發言人莫德·布雷容 (Maud Bregeon) 告訴 TF1 電視台，有七起死亡事件直接或間接與高溫有關，其中五起是溺水。美國國家公共廣播電台指出，許多溺水事件發生是因為人們試圖降溫，而熱浪來襲時，救生員尚未完全在熱門海灘值勤，增加了風險。

paragraphs.en: Governments are taking action to respond to the heat. In Italy’s Lazio region, authorities have placed restrictions on outdoor work, such as on farms and construction sites, between 12:30 PM and 4:00 PM, The Guardian World reported. This is similar to rules put in place last year, but they are starting earlier this year, DW Top added. In France, 13 of the country’s administrative departments have been placed on an orange high temperature alert, the second highest level, which means people need to be careful and take precautions, The Guardian World stated. This is the first time the national heat warning system has been activated in May since it began in 2004.
paragraphs.zh: 各國政府正在採取行動應對高溫。《衛報世界版》報導，在義大利的拉齊奧大區，當局已限制下午 12 點 30 分至 4 點之間在戶外工作，例如農場和建築工地。德國之聲補充說，這與去年實施的規定類似，但今年提早開始。根據《衛報世界版》報導，法國有 13 個行政區已發布橙色高溫警報，這是第二高警戒級別，意味著民眾需要小心並採取預防措施。這是自 2004 年國家高溫預警系統啟用以來，首次在五月份啟動。

paragraphs.en: Experts warn that such early and intense heat events are becoming more common. Robert Vautard, a climate researcher, told Agence France-Presse that this extension of the heatwave season is a clear sign of climate change effects. He added that similar heat events could eventually be seen in April and October, The Guardian World reported. Peter Thorne, director of the ICARUS Climate Research Centre, told NPR News that there is no doubt heatwave events like this are more likely and more severe due to climate change. He described many of the records being set as “mind-bogglingly crazy.” Christophe Cassou, a climate scientist, told Le Monde that this event has a “one in 1,000 chance of happening at this time of year in the climate of 1979 to 2025” and “would have been virtually impossible in the preindustrial era,” The Guardian World noted. This situation highlights the urgent need for countries to adapt their infrastructure, like schools and hospitals, to meet the conditions of a warming planet, as climate advisers warned the UK government last week, DW Top reported.
paragraphs.zh: 專家警告，這種提早且強烈的熱浪事件正變得越來越普遍。氣候研究員羅伯特·沃塔德 (Robert Vautard) 告訴法新社 (Agence France-Presse)，熱浪季節的延長是氣候變遷影響的明確跡象。他補充說，類似的熱浪事件最終可能在四月和十月出現，《衛報世界版》報導。ICARUS 氣候研究中心主任彼得·索恩 (Peter Thorne) 告訴美國國家公共廣播電台，毫無疑問，由於氣候變遷，像這樣的熱浪事件更有可能發生且更為嚴重。他形容許多正在創下的紀錄「令人難以置信地瘋狂」。氣候科學家克里斯托夫·卡蘇 (Christophe Cassou) 告訴《世界報》(Le Monde)，這次事件在「1979 年至 2025 年的氣候中，在一年中的這個時候發生的機率為千分之一」，並且「在工業化前時代幾乎不可能發生」，《衛報世界版》指出。德國之聲報導，這種情況凸顯了各國迫切需要調整其基礎設施，例如學校和醫院，以應對氣候變暖的條件，正如氣候顧問上週警告英國政府的那樣。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Used to emphasize that something is even less likely or possible than another thing mentioned.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 更不用說；何況（用於強調某事比前面提到的事更不可能或更難發生）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語用於強調語氣。文中：The Met Office described this heat as “exceptional even in the middle of summer, let alone spring.”

keyTerms.definitionEn: To break a record by a very large amount, or to break it very easily.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「打碎」或「撞擊」，這裡指「大幅打破紀錄」。文中：Many places have seen temperatures not just break but “smash” previous records.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have remained in existence or valid for a period of time.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （紀錄、規則等）維持不變；持續有效。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「站立」，這裡指紀錄「維持」或「保持」不變。文中：This broke the previous May record of 32.8°C, which had stood since 1922.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A public holiday when banks and most businesses are closed.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 銀行假日；國定假日（英國等國家的公共假日，銀行和大多數商店會關閉）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此為特定文化背景下的公共假日名稱。文中：The National Fire Chiefs Council also issued a water safety warning after several deaths over a bank holiday weekend.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Extremely surprising, confusing, or difficult to imagine.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 令人難以置信地；極其驚人地。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此副詞強調程度之高，表示非常令人震驚或難以理解。文中：He described many of the records being set as “mind-bogglingly crazy.”

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Why temperature records are being not only broken but smashed

sources.name: The Guardian Environment
sources.title: UK records highest ever May temperature for second day in a row

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Seven heat-related deaths in France as May records set in several countries

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: An exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Unseasonal May heat wave grips Europe

quiz.question: Which of the following countries broke its May temperature record by more than 2°C this year?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 B，因為文章第二段明確提到：「根據BBC新聞報導，英國週二氣溫超過35°C，比今年之前的五月紀錄高出2°C以上。」這直接回答了問題。選項 A 不對，因為法國是打破了數百個高溫紀錄，但文章沒有具體說明是「超過2°C」。選項 C 不對，因為愛爾蘭的五月氣溫紀錄是被打破「超過1°C」，而非2°C。選項 D 不對，因為德國僅被提及「異常炎熱」，沒有具體說明打破紀錄的幅度。

quiz.question: Based on the article, what can be inferred about the effectiveness of current heatwave preparedness measures in Europe?

options.A: Existing measures are generally sufficient, as evidenced by the low number of heat-related deaths.
options.B: Governments are implementing new, comprehensive strategies that completely prevent heat-related incidents.
options.C: Despite some governmental actions, the early and intense nature of this heatwave has exposed vulnerabilities, particularly regarding water safety and lifeguard availability.
options.D: The heatwave has had minimal impact on public health and safety, suggesting robust preparedness.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 C，因為文章第五段提到：「國家消防總長理事會也發布了水域安全警告，因為在一個銀行假日週末發生了數起死亡事件。」以及「許多溺水事件的發生是因為人們試圖降溫，而熱浪來襲時，救生員尚未完全到位，增加了風險。」這表明儘管有政府行動（如健康警報），但熱浪的早期和強度仍然暴露了準備工作的不足之處，特別是在水域安全方面。選項 A 不對，因為文章明確提到了多起死亡事件，並非「低數量」。選項 B 不對，文章提到義大利的限制措施是類似去年的規定，只是提早實施，且法國的警報系統雖然啟動，但並沒有說這些是「完全防止」事件發生的「新、全面策略」。選項 D 不對，因為文章詳細描述了多起死亡事件和健康風險，表明影響並非「最小」。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, the word "smash" is used to describe how temperatures broke previous records. What does "smash" most closely mean in this context?

options.A: To break by a significant margin; to greatly exceed.
options.B: To break with difficulty; to barely surpass.
options.C: To break accidentally; to unintentionally exceed.
options.D: To break into many pieces; to shatter physically.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 A，文章第四段提到：「許多地方的氣溫不僅打破了，而且『粉碎 (smash)』了之前的紀錄。」接著舉例英國倫敦氣溫達到35.1°C，打破了自1922年以來32.8°C的紀錄，這是一個顯著的差距。因此，'smash' 在此語境中表示以巨大的幅度打破或遠遠超過。選項 B 不對，因為它強調「困難地」或「勉強地」，這與「粉碎」所表達的強烈程度相反。選項 C 不對，因為「意外地」或「無意中」不符合描述氣溫紀錄的語境。選項 D 不對，因為「粉碎成碎片」是指物理上的破壞，與氣溫紀錄無關，是字面意義的誤用。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: Western Europe is experiencing a typical early summer heatwave that is well within historical norms.
options.B: While heatwaves are occurring, their intensity and frequency are not significantly influenced by climate change.
options.C: Governments in Western Europe have successfully implemented all necessary measures to fully protect citizens from the impacts of severe heatwaves.
options.D: Western Europe is facing an unprecedented, early, and intense heatwave, largely exacerbated by human-caused climate change, highlighting the urgent need for adaptation.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 D，這篇文章的中心主旨是描述西歐正在經歷一場「異常強烈且提前」的熱浪，多個國家創下五月高溫紀錄，並強調「科學家們明確指出，人類造成的氣候變遷，主要是燃燒煤炭、石油和天然氣，使得這次熱浪更加劇烈。」文章也提到專家警告此類事件將更頻繁，並強調「迫切需要各國調整其基礎設施」。選項 A 不對，因為文章多次強調這次熱浪是「不尋常的」、「前所未有的」。選項 B 不對，因為文章明確指出氣候變遷是導致熱浪更強烈的原因。選項 C 不對，儘管文章提到政府正在採取行動，但也指出熱浪造成了多起死亡事件，並暴露了準備工作的不足，因此「完全保護」的說法不符事實。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
